description: Connects the modulus/remainder framework (Sessions 1-4) to the dual geometry hypothesis (Session 8). The modulus is the spherical beta content (pi powers from angular integrations). The remainder is the beta^0 piece — previously treated as opaque number theory. This experiment tests whether the remainders contain elliptic structure (toroidal geometry), which would complete the decomposition: every QED coefficient = spherical modulus + toroidal remainder.
thesis: The modulus/remainder decomposition is the spherical/toroidal decomposition. The modulus (beta^2, beta^4) is the spherical angular content. The remainder (beta^0) contains both number-theoretic constants (zeta, Li — known) and toroidal-geometric constants (elliptic K, E — the Laporta integrals). At loops 1-3, the remainder is entirely number-theoretic. At loop 4, the remainder includes toroidal-geometric content for the first time.
formula: A_n = modulus(beta^2k terms) + remainder(beta^0 terms) = spherical + (number-theoretic + toroidal)
